entity.name: openZro Project
entity.description: openZro is an open-source, community-maintained platform for zero-trust mesh networking on top of WireGuard. It is a BSD-3-Clause fork of NetBird, maintained independently of any corporate vendor. The project ships a self-hosted control plane (management, signal, relay, dashboard) plus a Dex-based identity layer, and aims to keep advanced mesh networking + per-resource access control accessible to teams that don't have a multi-million dollar IT budget.

projects.name: openZro
projects.description: openZro is a configuration-free peer-to-peer private network and a centralized access control system combined in a single open-source platform. It makes it easy to create secure WireGuard-based private networks for your organization or home. Community-maintained fork of NetBird, licensed under BSD-3-Clause.

channels.description: Contact the maintainers at dev@openzro.io for bank transfer details.
